metadata.title: ATS Job Scraper and Job Board Platform: How We Built It

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Every ATS scraper follows a proven two-phase architecture for collecting job data. Scraped listings are stored in a NoSQL database and served through a public job board. The frontend uses React or Next.js with routing, state, search, and faceted filters. Greenhouse and Lever alone cover most visa-sponsoring tech companies students target.

artifactLines: Every ATS scraper follows a proven two-phase architecture for collecting job data.
Scraped listings are stored in a NoSQL database and served through a public job board.
The frontend uses React or Next.js with routing, state, search, and faceted filters.
Greenhouse and Lever alone cover most visa-sponsoring tech companies students target.

beats.narration_text: The two-phase scraper architecture and the fact that Greenhouse and Lever cover 60 to 70 percent of visa-sponsoring tech companies made this feel buildable. I want the mechanics. Explain to me: (1) what the two phases of an ATS scraper actually do step by step, (2) why NoSQL fits the scraped job data better than a relational schema here, and (3) how the faceted filter and full-text search would let an international student isolate only visa-sponsoring roles.

beats.narration_text: Task 6.2 - Frontend Framework Setup: Initialize frontend (React / Next.js recommended), configure routing, state management. Task 6.6 - Search & Filter Engine: Full-text search on title + description, faceted filters, sort by date/relevance.

beats.narration_text: Primary Use Case: International students searching for jobs at companies that sponsor work visas. Greenhouse and Lever alone cover an estimated 60–70%+ of visa-sponsoring tech companies, while enterprise ATS systems like Workday, iCIMS, and Taleo cover the Fortune 500 segment.
